messages.content: 你是一位资深临床医学编辑，专注于面向临床医生的循证医学内容评估与改进。请严格按照要求的JSON格式输出，不要输出任何其他内容。

请对【非甲状腺病态综合征】知识库词条「鉴别诊断」章节的质量分析结果进行二次校验与优化。

## 章节原文内容


## 一、 鉴别诊断
非甲状腺疾病综合征（nonthyroidal illness syndrome，NTIS）的最重要特点是血清促甲状腺激素（thyroid stimulating hormone，TSH）降低，因而 NTIS 的鉴别其实就是低 TSH 血症的鉴别，在临床上，遇到 TSH 降低时，不能仅根据下丘脑-垂体-甲状腺的长负反馈原理而武断为甲亢或 NTIS，仅仅在该两种情况之间进行鉴别，因为许多临床情况和药物均可导致血清 TSH 降低，如中枢性甲减、甲状腺炎、TSH 非平衡状态、药物、肝细胞性肝癌（hepatocellular carcinoma，HCC）升高等，甚至偶尔亦见于正常人。与原发性或继发性甲减的鉴别血 TSH 降低的鉴别在 NTIS 中，T₃、T₄异常的严重性预示着患者的预后。在 NTIS 中，临床型甲减也难以诊断。

### (一) 桥本甲状腺炎或甲状腺功能减退症

### 1、 甲状腺功能减退症

### (1) TSH 水平明显升高可能提示既往存在甲状腺功能减退症。如果 TSH 在 25-30 mU/L 以上极可能为原发性甲减，约 12% 的 NTIS 患者 TSH 在正常以上，不足 3% NTIS 患者的 TSH 在 20 mU/L 以上。未用抑制 TSH 分泌药物的 NTlS 患者，血游离三碘甲状腺原氨酸（free triiodothyronine，FT₃）降低强烈提示为甲减，但反三碘甲状腺原氨酸（reverse triiodothyronine，rT₃）对甲减诊断无帮助。继发性甲减的血 TSH 可能降低、正常或轻度升高。

### (2) 除了血清促甲状腺激素浓度升高外，血清三碘甲状腺原氨酸/甲状腺素比值相对较高、甲状腺激素结合率较低以及血清重组三碘甲状腺原氨酸浓度较低，均提示甲状腺功能减退症而非非甲状腺疾病综合征，反之亦然。然而，许多医院并未常规开展 rT3 检测，因此 rT3 检测无法可靠地区分甲状腺功能减退症和非甲状腺疾病综合征。

### (3) 抗甲状腺过氧化物酶（thyroid peroxidase antibody，TPO）抗体滴度阳性有助于原发性甲状腺功能减退症的诊断，但不能确诊。

### 2、 中枢性甲状腺功能减退症

### (1) 如果 NTIS 患者无垂体或下丘脑疾病，血皮质醇常正常或升高，而血催乳素（prolactin，PRL）促性腺激素正常。相反，如果血皮质醇、促性腺激素降低而 PRL 升高则支持中枢性（垂体或下丘脑性）损伤。NTIS 患者的甲状腺疾病诊断最好在急性恢复后进行。

### (2) 在某些患者中，尤其是在幼儿中，区分 NTIS 和中枢性甲状腺功能减退症可能具有挑战性。区分这些导致甲状腺激素水平低下的原因至关重要，因为目前的医疗实践是，对于 NTIS 患者不进行甲状腺素补充治疗，而一旦确诊为中枢性甲状腺功能减退症，则应迅速开始甲状腺素补充治疗，以确保最佳的大脑发育。

### (二) 甲状腺功能亢进症或甲状腺毒症
1、 NTIS 患者的甲状腺疾病诊断有一定困难，这时的甲亢患者血总甲状腺素（total thyroxine，TT₄）和总三碘甲状腺原氨酸（total triiodothyronine，TT₃）可能正常，然而血 游离甲状腺素（free thyroxine，FT₄）和 FT₃仍有诊断价值。甲亢时血 TSH 多不可测得（<0.10 mU/L）。但在 NTIS 中仅 7% 以下的患者 TSH 不可测得（常见于多巴胺和糖皮质激素治疗患者）。
2、 多数甲亢患者虽然血清三碘甲状腺原氨酸（triiodothyronine，T₃）和甲状腺素（thyroxine，T₄）均增高，但血 T₃增高较明显。提示甲亢时甲状腺释放较多 T₃及末梢组织将 T₄转化为 T₃增加。
3、 亚临床 T₄型甲亢是指血清有较明显增高，而血 T₃大致正常为特点的一种甲亢类型，主要见于既往过多暴露于碘的老年人。若无过量碘摄入史，多提示外周组织 T₄转化为 T₃受抑制，而高综合征时血 rT₃升高；TSH 正常可鉴别。

### (三) 全垂体功能减退症
对于临床上病情不严重的儿童，必须将 NTIS 与垂体功能不全相鉴别。在垂体功能不全中，其他垂体激素，例如生长激素、促肾上腺皮质激素（adrenocorticotrophic hormone，ACTH）、黄体生成素和卵泡刺激素，也可能不足，导致幼儿生长发育迟缓。除了游离 T₄ 和 TSH 水平偏低外，胰岛素样生长因子（insulin-like growth factors，IGF-1） 水平也显著降低。

### (四) 药物引起的甲状腺功能障碍
这些药物种类繁多例如但不限于胺碘酮治疗、多巴胺、类固醇、锂、碘化物、雌激素或雄激素的使用，以及抗癫痫药或阿片类药物。

## 第一次分析识别到的问题（共15项）

  1. [内容缺失/高优先]（重点内容） 鉴别诊断章节缺少对“甲状腺功能亢进症或甲状腺毒症”与NTIS鉴别诊断的核心鉴别点——TSH受体抗体（TRAb）或甲状腺刺激性免疫球蛋白（TSI）检测的讨论。这是临床鉴别甲亢与NTIS的关键实验室指标，属于重点内容缺失。
    示例：章节中“甲状腺功能亢进症或甲状腺毒症”部分仅讨论了TSH、T3、T4、rT3，未提及TRAb或TSI检测。
    原文定位：1、 NTIS患者的甲状腺疾病诊断有一定困难，这时的甲亢患者血总甲状腺素（total thyroxine，TT₄）和总三碘甲状腺原氨酸（total triiodothyronine，TT₃）可能正常，然而血 游离甲状腺素（free thyroxine，FT₄）和 FT₃仍有诊断价值。甲亢时血 TSH 多不可测得（<0.10 mU/L）。但在 NTIS 中仅 7% 以下的患者 TSH 不可测得（常见于多巴胺和糖皮质激素治疗患者）。
2、 多数甲亢患者虽然血清三碘甲状腺原氨酸（triiodothyronine，T₃）和甲状腺素（thyroxine，T₄）均增高，但血 T₃增高较明显。提示甲亢时甲状腺释放较多 T₃及末梢组织将 T₄转化为 T₃增加。
3、 亚临床 T₄型甲亢是指血清有较明显增高，而血 T₃大致正常为特点的一种甲亢类型，主要见于既往过多暴露于碘的老年人。若无过量碘摄入史，多提示外周组织 T₄转化为 T₃受抑制，而高综合征时血 rT₃升高；TSH 正常可鉴别。
    指南依据：参考数据源 4：2022 Euthyroid Sick Syndrome - StatPearls - NCBI Bookshelf.pdf：Differential diagnoses of euthyroid sick syndrome include: Hashimoto thyroiditis or hypothyroidism; Hyperthyroidism or thyrotoxicosis; Panhypopituitarism; Thyroid dysfunction is induced by the use of different drugs...
  2. [内容缺失/中优先] 鉴别诊断章节缺少对“药物引起的甲状腺功能障碍”与NTIS鉴别的具体鉴别要点。章节仅列举了药物种类，未说明如何通过病史、用药时间、甲状腺功能模式等与NTIS区分，属于非重点内容缺失但影响临床实用性。
    示例：章节中“(四) 药物引起的甲状腺功能障碍”仅列出药物名称，无任何鉴别方法。
    原文定位：(四) 药物引起的甲状腺功能障碍
这些药物种类繁多例如但不限于胺碘酮治疗、多巴胺、类固醇、锂、碘化物、雌激素或雄激素的使用，以及抗癫痫药或阿片类药物。
    指南依据：参考数据源 4：2022 Euthyroid Sick Syndrome - StatPearls - NCBI Bookshelf.pdf：Differential diagnoses of euthyroid sick syndrome include: ... Thyroid dysfunction is induced by the use of different drugs through various mechanisms; the list of these medications is long, and some examples include but are not limited to, amiodarone therapy, dopamine, steroids, lithium, iodide, estrogen or androgen use, as well as antiseizure or opioid meds
  3. [内容缺失/高优先]（重点内容） 鉴别诊断章节缺少对“全垂体功能减退症”与NTIS鉴别的关键鉴别点——促肾上腺皮质激素（ACTH）刺激试验或皮质醇水平。章节仅提到血皮质醇正常或升高、PRL和促性腺激素变化，但未提及ACTH刺激试验在鉴别中的价值，属于重点内容缺失。
    示例：章节中“(三) 全垂体功能减退症”部分未提及ACTH刺激试验。
    原文定位：(三) 全垂体功能减退症
对于临床上病情不严重的儿童，必须将 NTIS 与垂体功能不全相鉴别。在垂体功能不全中，其他垂体激素，例如生长激素、促肾上腺皮质激素（adrenocorticotrophic hormone，ACTH）、黄体生成素和卵泡刺激素，也可能不足，导致幼儿生长发育迟缓。除了游离 T₄ 和 TSH 水平偏低外，胰岛素样生长因子（insulin-like growth factors，IGF-1） 水平也显著降低。
    指南依据：参考数据源 3：Nonthyroidal Illness Syndrome in a Noncritically Ill Child.pdf：Before the start of thyroxine supplementation, adrenal insufficiency was excluded with a remarkably increased random cortisol measured at 13:30 PM of 0.91 μmol/L (= 33 μg/dL, reference interval 0.09–0.81 μmol/L). Therefore, no additional low-dose adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH) test was performed.
  4. [内容缺失/中优先] 鉴别诊断章节缺少对“桥本甲状腺炎或甲状腺功能减退症”与NTIS鉴别的关键鉴别点——甲状腺超声或甲状腺自身抗体（TPOAb、TgAb）的详细讨论。章节仅提到TPO抗体滴度阳性有助于诊断，但未讨论TgAb、甲状腺超声在鉴别中的价值，属于非重点内容缺失。
    示例：章节中“(一) 桥本甲状腺炎或甲状腺功能减退症”部分仅提及TPO抗体，未提及TgAb或甲状腺超声。
    原文定位：(3) 抗甲状腺过氧化物酶（thyroid peroxidase antibody，TPO）抗体滴度阳性有助于原发性甲状腺功能减退症的诊断，但不能确诊。
    指南依据：参考数据源 4：2022 Euthyroid Sick Syndrome - StatPearls - NCBI Bookshelf.pdf：Differential diagnoses of euthyroid sick syndrome include: Hashimoto thyroiditis or hypothyroidism
  5. [内容不准确/中优先] 内容不合理：在“(二) 甲状腺功能亢进症或甲状腺毒症”部分，第3点描述“亚临床 T₄型甲亢”时，出现“而高综合征时血 rT₃升高”的表述，其中“高综合征”含义不明，可能为笔误或术语错误，影响用户理解。
    示例：原文：而高综合征时血 rT₃升高
    原文定位：3、 亚临床 T₄型甲亢是指血清有较明显增高，而血 T₃大致正常为特点的一种甲亢类型，主要见于既往过多暴露于碘的老年人。若无过量碘摄入史，多提示外周组织 T₄转化为 T₃受抑制，而高综合征时血 rT₃升高；TSH 正常可鉴别。
  6. [内容不准确/中优先] 内容不合理：在“(一) 桥本甲状腺炎或甲状腺功能减退症”部分，第(2)点提到“血清重组三碘甲状腺原氨酸浓度较低”，其中“重组三碘甲状腺原氨酸”应为“反三碘甲状腺原氨酸（reverse T3, rT3）”的笔误或术语错误，与上下文及临床常识不符。
    示例：原文：血清重组三碘甲状腺原氨酸浓度较低
    原文定位：(2) 除了血清促甲状腺激素浓度升高外，血清三碘甲状腺原氨酸/甲状腺素比值相对较高、甲状腺激素结合率较低以及血清重组三碘甲状腺原氨酸浓度较低，均提示甲状腺功能减退症而非非甲状腺疾病综合征，反之亦然。
  7. [内容不准确/中优先] 内容不合理：在“(一) 桥本甲状腺炎或甲状腺功能减退症”部分，第(2)点提到“血清重组三碘甲状腺原氨酸浓度较低”，但同一章节前文已使用“反三碘甲状腺原氨酸（reverse triiodothyronine，rT₃）”，术语不统一，造成混淆。
    示例：前文：反三碘甲状腺原氨酸（reverse triiodothyronine，rT₃）；后文：血清重组三碘甲状腺原氨酸浓度较低
    原文定位：未用抑制 TSH 分泌药物的 NTlS 患者，血游离三碘甲状腺原氨酸（free triiodothyronine，FT₃）降低强烈提示为甲减，但反三碘甲状腺原氨酸（reverse triiodothyronine，rT₃）对甲减诊断无帮助。；(2) 除了血清促甲状腺激素浓度升高外，血清三碘甲状腺原氨酸/甲状腺素比值相对较高、甲状腺激素结合率较低以及血清重组三碘甲状腺原氨酸浓度较低，均提示甲状腺功能减退症而非非甲状腺疾病综合征，反之亦然。
  8. [语言/格式/低优先] 英文缩写格式问题：英文缩写首次出现时未给出中英文全称。例如“HCC”在正文中首次出现时未给出全称；“PRL”在正文中首次出现时未给出全称；“ACTH”在正文中首次出现时未给出全称。
    示例：1. HCC 未给出全称；2. PRL 未给出全称；3. ACTH 在“(三) 全垂体功能减退症”中首次出现时未给出全称（虽然在前文“(一) 桥本甲状腺炎或甲状腺功能减退症”中已出现，但该字段为鉴别诊断字段，按规范每个字段第一次出现均需补全称）。
    原文定位：肝细胞性肝癌（hepatocellular carcinoma，HCC）升高等；而血催乳素（prolactin，PRL）促性腺激素正常；在垂体功能不全中，其他垂体激素，例如生长激素、促肾上腺皮质激素（adrenocorticotrophic hormone，ACTH）、黄体生成素和卵泡刺激素，也可能不足
  9. [语言/格式/低优先] 错别字/笔误：正文中出现“NTlS”（字母l代替数字1或大写I），应为“NTIS”。
    示例：原文：未用抑制 TSH 分泌药物的 NTlS 患者
    原文定位：未用抑制 TSH 分泌药物的 NTlS 患者，血游离三碘甲状腺原氨酸（free triiodothyronine，FT₃）降低强烈提示为甲减
  10. [语言/格式/低优先] 语言不流畅/逻辑跳跃：在“一、 鉴别诊断”开篇段落中，句子“与原发性或继发性甲减的鉴别血 TSH 降低的鉴别在 NTIS 中，T₃、T₄异常的严重性预示着患者的预后。”存在语法错误和逻辑跳跃，读起来不通顺，影响理解。
    示例：原文：与原发性或继发性甲减的鉴别血 TSH 降低的鉴别在 NTIS 中，T₃、T₄异常的严重性预示着患者的预后。
    原文定位：与原发性或继发性甲减的鉴别血 TSH 降低的鉴别在 NTIS 中，T₃、T₄异常的严重性预示着患者的预后。
  11. [语言/格式/低优先] 语言不流畅/逻辑跳跃：在“(一) 桥本甲状腺炎或甲状腺功能减退症”部分，第(2)点中“血清三碘甲状腺原氨酸/甲状腺素比值相对较高、甲状腺激素结合率较低以及血清重组三碘甲状腺原氨酸浓度较低”与后文“均提示甲状腺功能减退症而非非甲状腺疾病综合征，反之亦然”之间的逻辑关系不清晰，且“反之亦然”指代不明。
    示例：原文：除了血清促甲状腺激素浓度升高外，血清三碘甲状腺原氨酸/甲状腺素比值相对较高、甲状腺激素结合率较低以及血清重组三碘甲状腺原氨酸浓度较低，均提示甲状腺功能减退症而非非甲状腺疾病综合征，反之亦然。
    原文定位：除了血清促甲状腺激素浓度升高外，血清三碘甲状腺原氨酸/甲状腺素比值相对较高、甲状腺激素结合率较低以及血清重组三碘甲状腺原氨酸浓度较低，均提示甲状腺功能减退症而非非甲状腺疾病综合征，反之亦然。
  12. [语言/格式/低优先] 语言不流畅/逻辑跳跃：在“(二) 甲状腺功能亢进症或甲状腺毒症”部分，第3点中“亚临床 T₄型甲亢是指血清有较明显增高”缺少主语，应为“血清T₄有较明显增高”。
    示例：原文：亚临床 T₄型甲亢是指血清有较明显增高
    原文定位：3、 亚临床 T₄型甲亢是指血清有较明显增高，而血 T₃大致正常为特点的一种甲亢类型
  13. [语言/格式/低优先] 语言不流畅/逻辑跳跃：在“(四) 药物引起的甲状腺功能障碍”部分，句子“这些药物种类繁多例如但不限于胺碘酮治疗、多巴胺、类固醇、锂、碘化物、雌激素或雄激素的使用，以及抗癫痫药或阿片类药物。”缺少标点，读起来不顺畅。

Nonthyroidal Illness Syndrome in a
Noncritically Ill Child
Annemieke M. V. Evelein, MD, PhD, 1 Rachel J. van der Pol, MD, PhD, 2 Karen van Wessel, MD, 3 Mark Hoetjer, MD, 4
Miranda P . Dierselhuis, MD, PhD,5 Hanneke M. van Santen, MD, PhD 1,5
Nonthyroidal illness syndrome (NTIS) is an endocrine phenomenon most com-
monly known from critically ill children admitted to the intensive care unit.
NTIS is regarded as physiological adaptation during severe illness. It is char-
acterized by low free triiodothyronine and free thyroxine (T4) concentrations
with normal to low levels of thyroid-stimulating hormone in combination with
the clinical context of the child. Distinction with central hypothyroidism can be
diﬃ cult, especially in early childhood, where congenital hypopituitarism can
present as well.
Here we report NTIS in a noncritically ill boy, 10 months old in the outpa-
tient clinic, with failure to thrive. Due to the noncritical illness and age, suspi-
cion of congenital hypopituitarism was raised and thyroxine supplementation
was started. Growth hormone de ﬁciency could not be excluded. Corticotropic
axis was found suﬃ cient with very high levels of cortisol. After extensive diag-
nostics including a brain magnetic resonance imaging, which was normal, a
neuroblastoma was diagnosed. After surgical resection of the neuroblastoma,
thyroid function recovered and thyroxine supplementation was stopped
successfully.
This case demonstrates that NTIS can be present in young children with
severe underlying disease but who are not critically ill. It emphasizes the
importance of a thorough diagnostic workup in children with otherwise unex-
plained “central hypothyroidism.” When low free T4 is found with high corti-
sol, this is a red ﬂag for the pediatrician pointing to severe underlying disease.
INTRODUCTION
Nonthyroidal illness syndrome (NTIS), also known as the euthyroid sick syndrome,
may be encountered in critically ill children in the neonatal or pediatric intensive
care unit (ICU). It is thought to be a physiological adaptation of the body secondary
to severe illness, such as sepsis or cardiac surgery,
1,2 and has been associated with
mortality.3 Furthermore, NTIS is described in patients with starvation, such as ano-
rexia nervosa,4,5 and during chemotherapy.6 In some patients, especially in young
children, distinguishing NTIS from central hypothyroidism can be challenging.
Diﬀerentiating between these causes of low thyroid hormones is important as cur-
rent medical practice is to not treat with thyroxine supplementation in case of NTIS
but to start rapidly with thyroxine supplementation when diagnosed with central
hypothyroidism to ensure optimal brain development.
This case report describes NTIS in a young boy, seen at the outpatient clinic, who
was not critically ill but presented with failure to thrive and low thyroid hormone
levels. The case stresses the importance of the thorough diagnostic process needed
to understand the etiology of aberrant thyroid hormone levels and raises the ques-
tion how critically ill is critical for NTIS to develop.

CASE PRESENTATION
Our patient presented at the age of 10 months at the pedi-
atric outpatient clinic with failure to thrive. The medical his-
tory of the boy included viral wheezing, there was no use of
medicines, and he was vaccinated according to the national
vaccine program. At presentation his anthropometrics were
as follows: weight 7.1 kg, weight for age −2.6 standard
deviation score (SDS) (declined from −1.3 SDS at the age
of 5 months), height 69 cm, height for age −2.0 SDS
(declined from −0.85 SDS at the age of 5 months), and
weight for height −1.4 SDS (declined from −0.74 SDS at
the age of 5 months). His dietary intake was adequate,
including breastfeeding until the age of 6 months, followed
by formula feeding in addition to solid foods. The boy was
developing normally. There was a short history of diarrhea
for a couple of days at the initial presentation. At physical
examination the boy was playful; however, he was skinny,
with distended abdomen, without any other abnormalities.
Blood tests showed no signs of anemia or in ﬂammation but
decreased free thyroxine (T4) (8.98 pmol/L, reference
interval 10.0–21.0 pmol) with low normal thyroid-stimulat-
ing hormone (TSH) (0.89 mU/L, reference interval 0.35 –
4.50 mU/L) was found. Additional blood tests 1 week later
conﬁrmed central hypothyroidism (free T4 8.72 pmol/L,
TSH 1.12 mU/L). In addition, a normal reverse T3 (rT3) con-
centration (0.17 nmol/L, reference interval 0.15 –0.54
nmol/L), low insulin-like growth factor 1 (IGF-1) 1.3
nmol/L ( −3.3 SDS), and random cortisol of 0.31 μmol/L
was found (reference interval in the referring hospital:
0.07–0.29 μmol/L) (Table 1).
Under suspicion of congenital hypopituitarism, the boy
was referred to the pediatric endocrinologist. Additional
medical history reported no abnormalities with regard to
the newborn screening. The boy had normal male genitalia
with testicles in the scrotal position and without signs of a
micropenis. Central hypothyroidism was again con ﬁrmed
(free T4 9.0 pmol/L and TSH 0.92 mU/L) and thyroxine sup-
plementation was started parallel to further diagnostics.
Before the start of thyroxine supplementation, adrenal insuf-
ﬁciency was excluded with a remarkably increased random
cortisol measured at 13:30
PM of 0.91 μmol/L (= 33 μg/dL,
reference interval 0.09 –0.81 μmol/L). Therefore, no addi-
tional low-dose adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH) test
was performed.
Diﬀerential diagnosis of the boy ’s hypothyroidism
included central hypothyroidism (congenital or acquired,
isolated or with multiple pituitary de ﬁciencies) and NTIS
due to anorexia/weight loss. A cerebral magnetic reso-
nance imaging scan showed a normal anterior pituitary
gland without abnormalities of the pituitary stalk and
hypothalamic region. Because the diarrhea became more
profound, the boy was referred to the pediatric
gastroenterologist.
At the age of 13 months, at the pediatric gastroenterolo-
gist, the boy had increasing abdominal distention and was
having watery stools up to 3 times a day. Common causes of
childhood diarrhea such as celiac disease and intestinal
parasitic infections were excluded. Urinary excretion of
homovanillic acid (295.9 mmol/mol creatinine, reference
interval 8 –20 mmol/mol creatinine) and vanillylmandelic
acid (41.1 mmol/mol creatinine, reference interval 5 –
13 mmol/mol creatinine) was elevated, pointing toward
neuroblastoma. An abdominal ultrasound con ﬁrmed an
adrenal lesion with a diameter of 4.6 cm with calci ﬁcations.
Metaiodobenzylguanidine scanning was negative, but ﬂuo-
rodeoxyglucose positron emission tomography scanning
showed pathological uptake in the tumor, without any signs
for metastases. Biopsy con ﬁrmed the diagnosis of neuro-
blastoma. Tumor resection was performed, and because
of low-risk histology, no further treatment was needed.
One week after surgery, thyroxine suppletion was stopped
as NTIS was deemed likely as the causative factor for the

DISCUSSION
Our case illustrates the importance of understanding the
etiology of low thyroid hormones when found in a child.
The distinction between central hypothyroidism and
NTIS can be challenging, and children do not need to be
critically ill to develop NTIS; however, severe illness
may be underlying. Especially the combination of low free
T4, normal TSH, and elevated cortisol may point toward
NTIS instead of pituitary disease and needs further diag-
nostic workup.
NTIS is an adaptive physiological response of the body
secondary to severe or critical illness.
4,5 In NTIS, hypotha-
lamic thyrotropin-releasing hormone secretion is downre-
gulated and, due to changed activity of the liver
deiodinases, conversion of T4 into T3 is decreased with
increased conversion of T4 into rT3. The net result of these
changes is lower levels of TSH, lower levels of the periph-
erally active T3, and increased levels of the biochemically
inactive reversed T3.
4,5 In a clinically nonsevere ill child,
NTIS must be di ﬀerentiated from pituitary insu ﬃ ciency.
In the latter, other pituitary hormones may also be insu ﬃ -
cient, such as growth hormone, ACTH, luteinizing hormone,
and follicle-stimulating hormone, leading to failure to thrive
in young children. Although he was having diarrhea and fail-
ure to thrive, our patient was actively playing and seem-
ingly happy in the outpatient clinic, for which NTIS was
deemed less likely. In addition to low free T4 and TSH,
IGF-1 was also notably reduced. This signi ﬁcantly low
IGF-1 level contributed to the suspicion of pituitary insu ﬃ -
ciency as the most likely diagnosis at that time, leading to
the initiation of thyroxine treatment, although low concen-
trations of IGF-1 may also be found in children with NTIS. In
hindsight, also reﬂected by the remarkably elevated cortisol
levels, the boy was severely ill with neuroblastoma as the
underlying disease.
NTIS is known to occur in starvation, mostly described in
patients with anorexia nervosa.
7 Although the nutritional
intake of our patient was not decreased, the combination
of increased caloric needs due to the tumor and malabsorp-
tion leading to insu ﬃ cient caloric absorption and weight
loss will have contributed to the development of NTIS in
our patient. Another possible mechanism in our patient
additionally decreasing TSH concentrations could be the
elevated catecholamine production in the neuroblastoma,
mainly dopamine and its metabolites. It has been described
that exogenous dopamine agonists can suppress TSH secre-
tion via activation of D2 receptors on the pituitary thyro-
tropic cells.
4,8 We therefore hypothesize that increased
levels of endogenous dopamine in children with neuroblas-
toma could lower TSH as well.
This report of a child with a newly diagnosed neuro-
blastoma shows that NTIS can serve as one of the main
diagnostic clues. Previously, our team studied thyroid
hormone concentrations in children newly diagnosed
with cancer for up to 3 months of treatment.
9 In this
cohort, in contrast to our patient, NTIS was not present
in any of the 220 children in whom thyroid levels were
measured at cancer diagnosis, although in 33% of chil-
dren, an isolated elevated rT3 level was found at cancer
diagnosis. The di ﬀerence with our patient might be due
to the type of the underlying tumor, as in the study of
Lebbink et al,
9 children included were having hematologi-
cal malignancies, brain tumors, and/or sarcomas but no
neuroblastoma.
Although the diagnosis of NTIS is based on ﬁnding low
free T3 and free T4 concentrations, with an inadequately
normal TSH concentration combined with the clinical set-
ting and symptoms of the child, determination of rT3 can
help in diagnosing NTIS. In NTIS, an elevated rT3 is
expected; however, rT3 can be normal,
5 as was seen in
our patient. Aside from understanding the cause of the
low thyroid hormone levels, distinguishing true central
hypothyroidism from NTIS is important with regard to
treatment decisions as well. With regard to the decision
to treat hypothyroidism, standard clinical care is to treat
central hypothyroidism, because of the importance of being
euthyroid for neurodevelopment, metabolism, and longi-
tudinal growth. However, in the situation of NTIS, thyroxine
treatment is not recommended.
10 In our patient, thyroxine
supplementation was started, and given the severity of the
symptoms of the boy with failure to thrive, congenital cen-
tral hypothyroidism was thought to be likely. The option of
postponing treatment after the diagnostic process was ﬁn-
ished did not seem to outweigh the possible bene ﬁts for the
boy’s clinical condition.
Although this report only describes one single patient,
this clinical case stresses the importance of measuring thy-
roid hormones in a child with failure to thrive and, when
found aberrant, the need for extensive diagnostic workup
if NTIS is found. Distinguishing NTIS from central hypothy-
roidism can be challenging due to the nonspeciﬁc endocrine
function tests but is of great importance for the decision of
whether to treat hypothyroidism. Reverse T3 can be of
added value in diagnosing NTIS but is not always directly
available and is not obligatory increased in NTIS,
5 as seen
in our patient. Moreover, whereas NTIS is thought to occur
predominantly in children in the ICU, it can be a symptom of
a chronically severe illness in a seemingly cheerful child in
the outpatient clinic as well. Endocrine phenomena, such as
the currently presented NTIS and markedly increased cor-
tisol, should serve as red ﬂags for the pediatrician in the
diagnostic process.

Euthyroid Sick Syndrome
Kavitha Ganesan; Catherine Anastasopoulou; Khurram Wadud.
Last Update: December 8, 2022.
Continuing Education Activity
Euthyroid sick syndrome is also known as nonthyroidal illness syndrome. It refers to changes in
thyroid function tests administered in the inpatient or intensive care setting during critical illness.
It is not a true syndrome, and transient alterations in the hypothalamic-pituitary-thyroid axis are
present in about 75 percent of hospitalized patients. This condition is often seen in patients with
severe critical illness, deprivation of calories, and following major surgeries. The most common
hormone pattern in euthyroid sick syndrome is a low total T3 and free T3 levels with low or
normal T4 and thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) levels. This activity reviews when to consider
euthyroid sick syndrome and how to evaluate and manage the condition while emphasizing the
critical role of the interprofessional care team in caring for patients with euthyroid sick
syndrome.
Objectives:
Determine the pathophysiology of euthyroid syndrome.
Evaluate how to distinguish euthyroid sick syndrome from true pathology affecting the
thyroid.
Identify when euthyroid sick syndrome should receive further evaluation.
Communicate the need for an interprofessional team approach to caring for a patient with
euthyroid sick syndrome.
Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.
Introduction
The euthyroid sick syndrome, also known as nonthyroidal illness syndrome, refers to changes
seen in the patient's thyroid function tests administered in the medical intensive care unit during
episodes of critical illness. It is not a true syndrome, but it represents significant alterations in the
hypothalamic-pituitary-thyroid axis in about 75% of hospitalized patients. This condition is often
seen in patients with severe critical illness, deprivation of calories, and following major
surgeries. The most common hormone pattern in euthyroid sick syndrome is a low total T3 and
free T3 levels with low or normal T4 and thyroid-stimulating hormone levels.[1][2][3]
Etiology
Causes of euthyroid sick syndrome vary to include several critical illnesses of different etiology.
Pneumonia, starvation, anorexia nervosa, sepsis, stress, history of trauma like, for example, hip
fracture[4][5], cardiopulmonary bypass, myocardial infarction, malignancies, burns, organ
transplantations, congestive cardiac failure[6], hypothermia, inflammatory bowel disease,
cirrhosis, major surgery, renal failure, and diabetic ketoacidosis have all been correlated with this
condition.[7] The latest addition to this long list has been the reports about euthyroid sick
presentation after COVID-19 infection.[8][9] The discovery of low T3 levels in COVID-19
patients was also a predictive marker for poor prognosis, even in the early stages of the disease.
[10]

Epidemiology
The most common abnormality, a total T3 level reduction, occurs in about 40% to 100% of
cases. In hospitalized patients, 10% are found to have low TSH levels measured, an abnormality
unrelated to thyroid pathology. The highest incidence occurs in the most severely ill group. The
probability of death correlates with the level of serum total T4. When total T4 levels drop below
4 mcg/dL, the probability of death is approximately 50%, and when serum T4 levels are
measured below 2 mcg/dL, the probability of death reaches over 80%.[11][12]
Pathophysiology
There are many proposed mechanisms regarding the pathogenesis of euthyroid sick syndrome.
One cause suggested is when the presence of thyroid-binding hormone inhibitors in the serum
and different body tissues inhibits the binding of the thyroid hormone to the thyroid-binding
protein. The euthyroid sick syndrome is also caused by cytokines such as interleukin 1,
interleukin 6, tumor necrosis factor-alpha, and interferon-beta affecting the hypothalamus and
pituitary glands, thus inhibiting TSH, thyroid-releasing hormone (TRH), thyroglobulin (TG), T3,
and the thyroid-binding globulins (TBG) production. Cytokines were also thought to reduce the
activity of type 1 deiodinase and decrease the binding capacity of the T3 nuclear receptors.
The peripheral deiodinase activity type 1 is downregulated, and the central type 2 and type 3
deiodinase activities are up-regulated in critically ill patients. Several other mechanisms can
contribute to the inhibition of 5'-monodeiodination, causing a decrease in the concentration of
serum total T3 levels in patients with a nonthyroidal illness, such as high serum cortisol and the
use of exogenous corticosteroid therapy, as well as other medications like amiodarone and
propranolol.
Serum albumin binds to fatty acids and displaces thyroid hormones from the thyroid-binding
globulin. The fall in serum albumin level in euthyroid sick syndrome enhances the activity of
different competitors of T4 on thyroid-binding globulin. Aspirin and heparin drugs impair the
protein binding of the thyroid hormones, reducing total T3 and T4 levels and, subsequently,
temporarily elevating free T3 and T4 levels.
Histopathology
Histopathology of the euthyroid sick syndrome is associated with a reduced follicular size and
weight of the thyroid gland in chronically ill patients. Acute liver disease and chronic kidney
disease are associated with an increase in the volume of the thyroid gland. Patients with a history
of chronic alcoholism have been found to have a reduction in the volume of the thyroid and also
the development of fibrosis in the gland.
History and Physical
History and physical examination findings are specific to the etiologic factors, with no typical
findings seen due to euthyroid sick syndrome per se. The condition may also affect patients who
have preexisting thyroid issues, and coexisting euthyroid sick syndrome can mask the typical
physical examination findings of hypothyroidism and hyperthyroidism, so ultimately, special
attention is required to differentiate a purely thyroid-related pathology from a euthyroid sick
effect.
Evaluation
Euthyroid sick syndrome has been classified as (1) low T4 syndrome, (2) low T3 and low T4
syndrome, (3) high T4 syndrome, and (4) other abnormalities of the thyroid.
Low serum total T3 level is the most common abnormality in euthyroid sick syndrome, and it is
seen in about 70% of hospitalized patients. The serum level of reverse T3 (rT3) is increased in

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK482219/ 2/6
euthyroid sick syndrome, except in renal failure. Elevated rT3 level is predominantly due to
decreased activity of the type I iodothyronine 5'-monodeiodinase (deiodination of T4 to T3 and
rT3 to 3,3'-diiodothyronine).
Both low T3 and low T4 syndrome are usually observed in the most critically ill patients
admitted to intensive care units. Low serum total T4 correlates with a bad prognosis; thyroid
binding globulin level is usually within normal, and the free T4 index is low in those patients. A
thyroid-binding hormone inhibitor in the circulation has explained this combination of findings
in euthyroid sick syndrome.[13][14]
The free T4 level is reduced in euthyroid sick syndrome patients who had received treatment
with drugs like dopamine and steroids that promote decreased TSH levels. Serum thyroid binding
globulin level increases in patients with acute intermittent porphyria and chronic hepatitis,
causing normal free T4 and high serum total T4 levels. Total and free T4 concentrations are
increased in patients who have been treated or exposed to amiodarone and radiocontrast agents
such as iopanoic acid. These substances cause a decrease in the hepatic uptake of T4 and 5'
monodeiodination of T4 to T3, and they can potentially precipitate hyperthyroidism in patients
with an autonomous thyroid nodule by accelerating the Jod Basedow phenomenon.
HIV patients have unusual variations of thyroid function, causing an increase in the T4 and TBG
levels, decreases in the reverse T3 and rT3/T4 ratio, and normal T3 and T3/T4 ratio. No imaging
studies or thyroid biopsy are necessary or play any role in evaluating and managing the euthyroid
sick syndrome.
Treatment / Management
Thyroid hormone replacement is generally not needed in patients with euthyroid sick syndrome.
Treatment and management of underlying medical illness is the main focus; however, periodic
monitoring of thyroid function should be done while the patient is in the hospital. After discharge
from the hospital, thyroid function abnormalities may persist for several weeks. In a clinically
euthyroid patient, thyroid function tests should be repeated no earlier than 6 weeks after
hospitalization to confirm either overt thyroid dysfunction when there is a persistent TSH
abnormality or euthyroid sick syndrome when there is a normalization of the TSH and the T3
levels.[15] Suppose the thyroid labwork is ordered at any earlier time. In that case, the TSH level
might be found to be elevated, reflecting the recovery phase of the axis, and this can be
confusing and mixed up with overt hypothyroidism. A recent study showed that in children
undergoing cardiac surgery, using thyroid hormone preoperatively to prevent euthyroid
sick syndrome helped decrease some cardiac complications, especially those related to ischemia.
[16][17]
Differential Diagnosis
Differential diagnoses of euthyroid sick syndrome include:
Hashimoto thyroiditis or hypothyroidism
Hyperthyroidism or thyrotoxicosis
Panhypopituitarism
Thyroid dysfunction is induced by the use of different drugs through various mechanisms;
the list of these medications is long, and some examples include but are not limited to,
amiodarone therapy, dopamine, steroids, lithium, iodide, estrogen or androgen use, as well
as antiseizure or opioid meds
Prognosis

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK482219/ 3/6
Low serum T3 level is correlated with an increased length of hospital stay, intensive care unit
admission, and the need for mechanical ventilation in patients with acute heart failure. The serum
T4 value also correlates with the outcomes in critically ill patients; values under 2 microg/dL
have been associated with mortality rates over 80%.
In a recent study from China, an increased rT3 level was associated with worse outcomes, mostly
observed in the most severely ill patients.[18] In other recent reports of COVID-19-infected
patients, the presence of euthyroid sick pictures was associated with poor prognosis and worse
outcomes even in the early stages of the disease.[10][19] In patients with hip fractures, the
presence of euthyroid sick syndrome was associated with an increased need for blood
transfusions and the development of anemia after a surgical repair.[5]
Complications
Complications of euthyroid sick syndrome are mostly associated with the underlying cause of the
problem and not related to the malfunction of the thyroid gland per se. After the initial medical
problem that caused the euthyroid sick syndrome presentation is treated, the expectation is for
the thyroid tests to become normal again without any remnant thyroid function issues. Suppose
the patient has a known thyroid problem. In that case, every effort must be made to clarify the
current thyroid function status based on symptomatology and physical examination findings so
that it can be treated accordingly. 
Consultations
Depending on the underlying disease that might have been the triggering factor for the
presentation of euthyroid sick syndrome, different specialists need to treat the patients to help
them recover and resolve the initial problem. If in doubt, the thyroid function tests should be
evaluated by an endocrine team that can also make arrangements for potential patient follow-up
after they are discharged from the hospital to secure complete resolution of the thyroid test
abnormalities without missing the possibility of the presence of a real thyroid-related issue that
requires further specialized and timely treatment.
Deterrence and Patient Education
Patients need to be notified of the abnormal thyroid test findings and advised to follow up with
their primary care physician to retest their thyroid levels when they have completely recovered
from the acute illness. The recommended timing is usually at least 6 weeks after hospitalization.
If the patients present with persistently abnormal thyroid hormone levels after the initial medical
disease has been resolved, further workup of the thyroid function is warranted to clarify and
recognize any purely thyroid-related problems that need special evaluation and appropriate
treatment. 
Pearls and Other Issues
Two general guidelines are important in evaluating critically ill patients:
1. First, measure the TSH level only if there is a high clinical suspicion of thyroid
dysfunction. If the TSH level is abnormal, further workup should be done. If the TSH level
is greater than 20 microUnits/mL or if it is undetectable, euthyroid sick syndrome is less
likely to cause abnormal lab results, and overt thyroid dysfunction should be strongly
considered. 
2. Second, when serum TSH is not elevated, the euthyroid sick syndrome should be
considered even in patients with known thyroid disease and low serum-free T3 or T4
levels.

Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
Healthcare workers, including family clinicians and various specialists, should be aware of
euthyroid sick syndrome, which often presents in hospitalized patients, especially after major
surgery or other serious illnesses. The key is to manage the primary illness and not the
abnormalities seen in the thyroid hormone levels. The outlook for patients with euthyroid sick
syndrome depends on the underlying primary condition; as long as the primary disorder is
unresolved, patients continue to show an aberration in the thyroid function tests until full
resolution is achieved. This requires an interprofessional healthcare team that includes all
clinicians (including surgeons in those cases), where everyone involved in the patient's care is in
open communication with the rest of the team, leading to prompt recognition of the condition
and appropriate management. This interprofessional approach results in the best patient
outcomes.

**判定主体：** 专家（≥2位）+ 内容组
**合格阈值：** 根据重点内容缺失、非重点内容缺失、数据源缺失扣对应分数，重点内容扣分＜10分/万字，或累积扣分＜20分/万字
**优秀阈值：** 根据重点内容缺失、非重点内容缺失、数据源缺失扣对应分数，重点内容扣分＜5分/万字，或累积扣分＜10分/万字

**重点内容**（缺失须有权威数据源支撑才算问题，否则不计入）：
- 定义/概述（词条定位清晰）
- 典型临床表现
- 主要辅助检查
- 诊断标准/诊断
- 治疗方案（含主要药物治疗、手术适应症等）

**非重点内容**（需个性化判断该疾病是否需要）：
- 分类/分型、发病机制、病理生理、解剖
- 少见/次要临床特征、体征/病史、次要辅助检查
- 预后、预防

**数据源要求：**
- 关键内容须有权威来源（指南、Meta分析、大型RCT）
- 数据源缺失：缺1篇扣1分，依次累加

**扣分示例：**
- 临床表现中缺失某分型表现内容：扣1分（如伯基特淋巴瘤缺失免疫缺陷型表现、散发型腹水表现）
- 临床表现仅介绍病史采集，无具体症状内容：扣5分（如白癜风仅采集病史无具体病史及症状介绍）
- 二线治疗仅列举可选方法，未介绍方案如何选择：扣5分
- 某药物疗程说明缺失：扣1分（如泼尼松疗程缺失，不便于判断疗效）

**优秀阈值：** 根据临床思维和整合逻辑扣对应分数，累积扣分＜10分/万字

- 预后分层若能指导治疗，放在治疗章节优于放在预后（扣3分）

**整合逻辑**（扣1-3分/处）：
- 相关内容须合理归类，标题须能概括其下所有内容
- 分类内容放置字段须与其临床用途匹配（扣3分）
- 标题提取范围不能涵盖其下全部内容（扣1分）；例：中枢神经系统预防标题无法涵盖全部内容（包含治疗）

**判定主体：** 内容组
**合格阈值：** 根据内容错误、内容陈旧和内容不合理扣对应分数，重点内容扣分＜10分/万字，或累积扣分＜20分/万字
**优秀阈值：** 根据内容错误、内容陈旧和内容不合理扣对应分数，重点内容扣分＜5分/万字，或累积扣分＜10分/万字

**重点/非重点内容判定：**
- 重点内容（severity=high）：定义/概述、典型临床表现、主要辅助检查、诊断标准/诊断、治疗
- 非重点内容（severity=medium/low）：其余内容

**内容错误**（扣1分/处）：

- 合理的判定标准：是否影响到用户理解，以及是否符合临床思维。当文献不一致时，保留的内容应是自洽的、清楚的

- 文字与表格内容重叠冗余但又不完整（扣2分）；例：辅助检查中文本和表格互补又重叠，仅保留一种更合适
- 多参考资料药物剂量冲突时未列举说明（扣1分）

- 内容前后矛盾（扣1-3分）

## 维度四：内容精炼流畅

**判定主体：** 内容组
**合格阈值：** 根据重复文本、研究内容未精简、图表不恰当、超链接不恰当、错别字、语言不流畅等扣对应分数，累积扣分＜20分/万字
**优秀阈值：** 根据重复文本、研究内容未精简、图表不恰当、超链接不恰当、错别字、语言不流畅等扣对应分数，累积扣分＜10分/万字

**同类错误多次出现算为一处，每处扣0.5分，扣分项包括：**
- 不合理的重复性文本、同一观点反复表达
- 研究性内容未精简（试验性/实验性/有争议内容须精简；里程碑式研究可不精简）
- 专业名词错误、机翻痕迹
- 不符合中国人阅读习惯
- 非同类错别字、错误空格/空行/格式
- 前后结构不一致、专有名词不统一
- 英文缩写首次出现未给出中英文全称（格式：中文全称（english full name，EFN））
- 存在"本指南""本共识""专家组认为""我们认为"等字眼

**图/表固定编号引用规则：**
- AI 当前只接收章节文本，图片二进制本身无法随 prompt 传递；这不代表实际词条中没有图片。若原文中出现图题、图注、图片占位符、图片内容文字或“图 N/见图N/详见图 N”等引用，默认对应图片在实际词条中存在，不得因“未看到图片本身/只看到图注/图片无法传递给 AI”判定为图片缺失、内容无法理解或需要补充图片。
- 正文中保留“详见表 2”“详见表2”“详见图 3”“见图1”等固定图表编号引用，属于知识库词条内对已有图/表的正常引用，符合要求。
- 不得仅因出现“详见表/图 + 固定编号”判定为“交互式关联缺失”“超链接不恰当”“应删除固定表格编号引用”等格式问题。
- 只有当引用的图/表在正文中明显不存在、编号前后矛盾、或引用内容导致医学含义错误时，才可按实际问题评价。

**上下标格式规则：**
- 上下标不是评审内容；不得仅因复制/解析后上标或下标显示为普通字符而判定为格式错误或科学计数法错误。
- 例如表格单位中的 `10⁹/L` 被复制/解析为 `109/L`，不应作为质量问题报告；只有医学数值本身确有错误且不依赖上下标显示差异时，才可按内容准确性问题评价。

注：内容全面的重点扣分指is_key_content=true的缺失问题；内容准确的重点扣分指severity=high的准确性问题。


## 内容要求规范
# 医学知识库词条内容要求规范

## 一、内容结构要求

**总要求：**
- 多篇资料有同一子级内容时，选择内容新、权威性高、更全面的参考资料作为主体，缺失处由其他资料补充
- 多篇参考资料内容冲突时，以内容新、权威性高的为准；无法判断取舍时，需将参考资料名称及其内容均写入系统，由审核/终审环节判断
- 多篇参考资料的内容请合理融合，先把握大致框架，再把握细节融合，做到语言通顺、逻辑合理、不重复

### 基础知识字段（一级目录）
二级目录：定义/概述、病因/危险因素、发病机制、流行病学、解剖学、病理生理学、分类/分型/分期
- 一般按上述顺序列举；特殊情况可调整（如病因按分类介绍时，分类放在病因前面更合适）
- 定义/概述必须有
- 分类/分型/分期的放置规则：
  - 与诊断、治疗相关（如肿瘤TNM分期、烧伤分度、危险度分级）→ 放"诊断"字段
  - 按临床表现的分类 → 放"诊断-临床表现"
  - 按影像学/内镜的分类 → 放"诊断-辅助检查"
  - 不涉及诊断、治疗的分类 → 放"基础知识"
- 流行病学：中国数据（如果数据源里有相关流行病学数据）全部保留。一般不保留预后的数据（如死亡率、生存情况等）。
- 疾病命名演变历史可保留，疾病发现历史不保留

### 诊断字段（一级目录）
二级目录：临床表现、辅助检查、诊断、病情评估、诊断注意事项（无需强行添加）、并发症/合并症
- 一般来说，临床表现、辅助检查、诊断三项必须有，但是要根据词条的情况，有的词条不需要描述临床表现，或者有的专注于治疗，则没有诊断的内容。
- 临床表现包括：病史、症状、体格检查，以及按临床症状分类/分型的内容
- 辅助检查包括：实验室检查、影像学检查、心电图、肺功能、有创检查、病理检查、分子/基因检测等；涉及微生物时可单独设置病原学检查（涂片镜检、培养、抗原检测、抗体检测、分子核酸检测等）
- 有些参考资料将症状、体征、辅助检查等混在一起命名为诊断，若内容无法拆分或拆分有违原意，可维持原有内容和标题不变（比较少见，尽量拆分）

### 鉴别诊断字段（一级目录）
内容集中在鉴别点，包括有相似临床表现、辅助检查的疾病鉴别，以及不同疾病的鉴别

### 治疗字段（一级目录）
二级目录（参考）：治疗原则、药物治疗、手术治疗、疗效评估、治疗注意事项（无需强行添加）
- 不得将"治疗"作为子级标题；应以具体治疗类型（"药物治疗""手术治疗"等）为子级标题
- 可按疾病分期/分型列举治疗方案（如"Ⅰ期肺癌治疗"）；总原则为按该疾病治疗的最优分层列举
- 参考资料中治疗原则和药物/手术/控制目标/注意事项混合时，需抽丝剥茧，将内容分块分级整理，使条理分明
- 多参考资料治疗标题不一致时，选择结构更清晰、更符合临床实际的参考资料作为框架，将其他参考资料对应内容融合至该框架下
- 多参考资料同一药物相同适应症相同剂型剂量不一致时，须列举各来源及其剂量
- 多参考资料推荐等级不一致时，或一线/二线推荐药物名称不同时，须列举各来源及推荐等级
- 按临床表现分不同治疗方案时，小标题应为临床表现（如"表现为急性肾损伤的治疗""表现为肾病综合征的治疗"）

### 控制目标（一级目录，非必有）
慢性疾病控制目标、急性疾病控制目标

### 预后字段（一级目录）
出院依据、预后、长期/短期监测、随访（包括患者教育）
- 若随访内容涉及治疗方案调整，建议放治疗字段

### 预防字段（一级目录）
筛查、预防，进行合理分层

## 二、格式规范

### 图/表规范
- 注意，图片只是无法传递给 AI/在文本中不显示，但实际词条中是有图片的；不得因为只看到图题、图注、图片占位符或图片内容文字，就判定图片缺失或需要补充图片。
- 注释中不保留图片来源署名（如"由某某提供""某某改编""来源于某某医院"等）
- 正文中允许保留“详见表 2”“详见表2”“详见图 3”“见图1”等固定编号图/表引用；这类引用不是交互式关联或超链接问题，不需要删除。只有图/表明显不存在、编号矛盾或医学含义错误时，才按实际问题处理。

### 英文缩写
- 首次出现时给出中英文全称，格式：社区获得性肺炎（community acquired pneumonia，CAP）
- 表格中出现的英文缩写，全称写在注释处
- 基础知识、诊断、治疗各字段中，每个字段第一次出现时均需补全称

### 标题要求
- 标题不可为英文（除特殊情况，如"SAPHO综合征"），中英文全称及缩写需转移至正文
- 标题须起提纲作用，无法提炼时可不设标题，不要硬设置

### 参考文献
- 参考文献序号不是评审内容

### 上标、下标
- 上下标不是评审内容；不得仅因复制/解析后上标或下标显示为普通字符而扣分。
- 例如表格单位中的 `10⁹/L` 被复制/解析为 `109/L`、化学式或指标中的下标被复制/解析为普通数字时，不应作为“格式错误”“科学计数法错误”报告；只有医学数值本身确有错误且不依赖上下标显示差异时，才按内容准确性问题评价。

## 三、内容要求

### 禁止用语
- 不得保留"本指南""本共识""专家组认为""我们认为"等字眼，但可以出现具体的某个指南建议的字样，如“2021版慢性阻塞性肺疾病诊治指南建议”、“文献报道”，可出现“本词条”、“本品”、“本剂”等词。

### 中医/中药内容
- 一般不建议保留，有很强的循证证据支持的酌情保留

### 研究性内容处理
- 使用总分结构：总结性意见放最前面，再分层描述各项研究
- 只保留结论性语句，包含时间、人物、研究性质、结论、参考文献；研究细节及方法可删除
- 格式：XX年XX[文献序号]的meta分析结果显示XXX
- 多个研究论证同一观点时，只保留一个结论，插入多篇参考文献
- 已有总述性结论且后续研究意思一致时，删除重复的细节研究
- 同一小标题下同类研究过多时，选择性保留新的、循证等级高的、里程碑意义的研究（不确定时建议全部保留，由审核/终审决定）

### 分层规则
- 不要以单独一句或半句话作为分层标记，避免分层太多太细打乱原有段落结构
- 分层原则：根据内容结构拆解，每部分表达同一层意思，可概括为一个中心，呈总分结构
- 例：介绍一个药物时可分为适应症、相关研究、剂量、注意事项等

### 多源内容融合规则
- 以内容新、权威性高、更全面的资料作为主体框架；缺失处由其他资料补充
- 内容冲突时以更新、权威性更高者为准；无法判断时两者均保留并注明来源
- 合并后做到语言通顺、逻辑合理、不重复
- 并非将新文献替换旧文献，而是选择合理框架、保证完整性、尽量融合

### 取舍规则
- 同一药物同适应症不同参考资料剂量不同时，均保留并标注来源
- 同类内容冲突时，理解内容意思后进行取舍，有冲突须在理解基础上保留合理内容

### 旧内容更新规则
- 旧内容错误或与新资料相悖：根据发表时间、权威性判断保留，计划删除的先标删除线等审核同意后再删；无法判断时两者均保留，写清各句话的来源
- 旧内容与新资料意思重复但表达略有不同：采用表达更合理的内容，可将旧内容标记删除线待审核
- 新资料内容是旧内容缺失且需补充的：补充至系统并标记颜色，参考资料中同步标记相同颜色
- 旧内容是新资料缺失且需保留的：标记为另一种颜色，在备注中说明
- 颜色选择原则：文字颜色尽量选择深色，背景颜色尽量选择浅色，以舒适不伤眼睛为准


请对上述每个问题逐一核查：
1. **核实**：对照章节原文，确认该问题是否真实存在，有无被原文实际内容覆盖
2. **优化**：若问题描述不够精准，基于原文修正描述并补充具体原文依据
3. **删除**：若某问题在原文中已有明确覆盖，或描述明显有误，则从结果中移除；特别地，若structure类问题指向的是"缺少某子级目录"，但该章节原始内容本身确实不涉及该内容（即并非内容存在却放错位置），则应删除该问题——内容框架中的子级目录为参考框架，不强制要求，不得因内容本身不存在而扣分
4. **合并**：若多个问题高度重叠，合并为一条更准确的描述
5. **补全指南依据**：若保留missing_content、accuracy或outdated问题，且该问题依据上传参考数据源/重点指南判断，必须在guideline_evidence中逐字摘录指南原文；若第一次分析已给出指南依据，默认保留，不得删除

参考文献序号核查规则（非常重要）：
- 不要将章节原文中的参考文献序号（如[53]、[54]、[444-445]）与参考数据源/指南文末参考文献列表中的编号建立对应关系；二者编号体系彼此独立
- 不得仅因原文引用序号与指南参考文献列表编号不一致，判定为“引用错误”“内容不准确”“文献引用序号错误”
- 评估准确性时只比较医学内容本身是否与指南正文观点一致，忽略原文引用序号

图/表固定编号引用核查规则（非常重要）：
- AI 当前只接收章节文本，图片二进制本身无法随 prompt 传递；这不代表实际词条中没有图片。
- 若章节原文中出现图题、图注、图片占位符、图片内容文字或“图 N/见图N/详见图 N”等引用，默认对应图片在实际词条中存在，不得因“未看到图片本身/只看到图注/图片无法传递给 AI”判定为图片缺失、内容无法理解或需要补充图片。
- “详见表 2”“详见表2”“详见图 3”“见图1”等固定图表编号引用，属于知识库词条内对已有图/表的正常引用，符合要求。
- 不得仅因原文保留“详见表/图 + 固定编号”而判定为“交互式关联缺失”“超链接不恰当”“应删除固定编号引用”“不符合知识库交互关联规范”等问题。
- 只有当被引用的图/表在章节原文或词条中明显不存在、编号前后矛盾，或引用内容导致医学含义错误时，才可按实际问题报告。
- 解析历史数据时，`[图注] 表 N ...` 也可能是表格标题；若其后紧跟 `|` 分隔的行、Markdown表格行或其他表格行，应视为该表已有具体表格内容，不得仅因标记为`[图注]`而报告“只有表题/未提供表格具体内容/无法识别表格信息”。

上下标格式核查规则（非常重要）：
- 上下标不是评审内容。由于复制/解析可能丢失 HTML 上标/下标格式，不得仅因 `10⁹/L` 显示为 `109/L`、下标显示为普通数字等情况，判定为“格式错误”“科学计数法错误”“单位指数错误”。
- 对表格单位、检验指标单位、化学式、基因/蛋白标记等场景，若问题仅依赖上下标显示差异，应删除该问题或不报告。
- 只有医学数值、单位含义或正文结论本身确有错误，且不是复制/解析导致的上下标显示差异时，才可按内容准确性问题报告。

原文边界核查规则（非常重要）：
- 只审查“章节内容（含子章节）/章节原文内容”中的知识库正文，不得把内容质量审评标准、内容要求规范、参考数据源或本提示词中的词句当成知识库原文问题。
- 对 style、accuracy、outdated 问题，必须能在章节原文中逐字定位到对应片段；如果无法在章节原文中定位，应删除该问题或不报告。
- 例如“本指南”“本共识”“专家组认为”等禁用词，只有真实出现在章节原文中才可报告；如果只出现在评审标准、内容规范或参考指南中，不得报告为原文问题。

标题序号层级核查规则（非常重要）：
- 标题序号常规顺序可为“一、→（一）→1、→（1）→1）→①→a.→I.”，但允许根据内容需要跳过中间层级。
- “一、”后面可以直接使用“1、”，不一定必须先出现“（一）”；不得仅因缺少“（一）”而判定标题层级混乱或结构问题。
- 只有在同一层级内部编号前后矛盾、标题与正文归属明显错位，或层级跳转导致用户无法理解内容关系时，才可报告结构问题。

以JSON格式输出最终核验结果：
{
  "verification_summary": "二次核验总结：说明做了哪些调整（删除/修正/合并了哪些问题）以及最终结论",
  "issues": [
    {
      "issue_type": "missing_content",
      "description": "核验后精准的问题描述（包含原文出处或缺失位置）",
      "severity": "high",
      "examples": ["原文片段或具体示例"],
      "anchors": [
        {"quote": "从章节原文中逐字摘录、最能定位该问题的连续片段", "heading_hint": "所在小标题，可为空"}
      ],
      "guideline_evidence": [
        {"source": "指南文件名或参考数据源编号", "quote": "指南原文中支持该判断的连续原文片段", "relevance": "说明该指南原文如何支持本问题"}
      ],
      "deduction_score": 5.0,
      "is_key_content": true
    }
  ]
}

issue_type取值：missing_content / accuracy / outdated / structure / style
severity取值：high / medium / low
anchors定位要求：每个问题尽量保留或补充1-3个quote，quote必须逐字摘录自章节原文；accuracy/outdated/style类问题必须提供对应原文片段；missing_content可提供最接近的标题或留空。
若一个问题包含多个具体小问题或多个具体例子（例如多个术语格式问题、多个重复位置、多个错误数据点），必须尽量为每个小问题分别提供一个anchor；每个anchor的quote应对应一个具体原文位置，避免只给一个总定位。若examples中列出“1. ...；2. ...；3. ...”等编号小点，anchors原则上不得少于这些编号小点数量，除非某个小点在章节原文中确实找不到对应片段。
guideline_evidence要求：若问题属于missing_content、accuracy或outdated，且判断依据来自参考指南/重点指南，必须填写1-3条guideline_evidence；source统一写“参考数据源 N：文件名”，N必须使用上传数据源的固定全局序号，即使该资料被标记为重点指南，也不要写“重点指南 N”；quote必须逐字摘录指南原文中支持该判断的连续片段，relevance说明该指南原文与问题的关系。若无法找到指南原文依据，不要把该项作为“与指南不相符/缺失/陈旧”问题保留。
若所有问题核验后均属实且无需修改，原样输出并在verification_summary中说明「问题属实，无需调整」。
若所有问题均被推翻，输出 {"verification_summary": "...", "issues": []}
